title: Leucosedum dasyphyllum
intro: Leucosedum dasyphyllum, also known as Dalmatian sage or Lad's love, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and woody stems. Leucosedum dasyphyllum has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, such as borneol and camphor, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Leucosedum dasyphyllum is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
taxonomy: Leucosedum dasyphyllum is classified in the family Crassulaceae. It belongs to the genus Leucosedum and the order Saxifragales. This plant's classification is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships.
